query: I have an XGBoost binary classifier predicting diabetes onset from 8 clinical features. Patient 42 was predicted positive — explain that specific prediction, and also tell me which features matter most globally.
expected_behavior: Selects shap.TreeExplainer (the tree-specific explainer for XGBoost), not KernelExplainer or DeepExplainer
Distinguishes the two requests by recommending a per-instance waterfall (or equivalent local plot) for patient 42 AND a global summary (bar / beeswarm of mean(|SHAP|)) for overall importance
Names or specifies a background dataset (stratified sample, ≥ ~100 rows from train) as required input, rather than silently running with no background or with the entire training set
